mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier financial conditions into higher equity valuations by lowering discount rates and supporting risk appetite, so the S&P 500 should post positive 20-day forward returns when the policy rate is trending down.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average during periods when FEDFUNDS is declining for at least 3 consecutive months, or if the signal's hit rate falls below 50% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises sharply, it signals labor-market deterioration and weakening household income, which should depress near-term equity earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if episodes with a 0.3pp or larger 3-month rise in UNRATE are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if average post-trigger returns are not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates broadening payroll income and retail demand, which should lift earnings expectations and support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-RSAFS expansion signal does not outperform either series alone and produces negative or near-zero 20-day SPX returns in triggered months, with Rank IC below 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp downturn in HOUST marks a housing-demand contraction that leads broader cyclical demand and confidence lower, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by at least 10% from its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly-horizon SPX returns are positive in more than half of 10%+ HOUST drawdown episodes, or if the average 60-day forward return after trigger events is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in INDPRO with stable or falling CPIAUCSL indicates stronger real activity without an inflation shock, which should support equities through improving margin expectations and positive 60-day returns.
failure_condition: Reject if periods of rising INDPRO with flat-to-declining CPIAUCSL do not deliver above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term fails to improve predictive power versus INDPRO alone.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is accelerating while FEDFUNDS remains very low, real policy rates become more negative and the market anticipates eventual tightening; that combination should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation exceeds FEDFUNDS by at least 2 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if high-inflation/low-rate episodes are not followed by negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the interaction effect becomes indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: In a recessionary labor regime, persistent losses in PAYEMS confirm income erosion and demand destruction, so the equity market should underperform over the next week as earnings revisions turn negative.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines in 2 consecutive monthly releases
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive payroll declines are not followed by negative 5-day SPX returns with materially higher frequency than baseline, or if the effect disappears outside recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rebound in GDPC1 and RSAFS signals that broad final demand is improving across national income and consumer spending, which should lift earnings expectations and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if GDP-and-retail demand rebounds do not lead to above-average 60-day SPX returns, or if the joint signal loses predictive value once either proxy is removed.
